description:
Goal: decide when Compiler2 can replace the old compiler.

Grounding from current state:
- `fz-rh2.14` settled the native/backend seam: compiler2 now owns the JIT/AOT/native handoff through `NativeProgram(root)` plus the one world-owned compiler2 `Types`, and runtime-membership questions use the shared first-class `RuntimeTypePredicate`.
- The remaining migration uncertainty is therefore above that seam: source-surface parity, contract comparison against the current compiler oracle, and identifying obsolete old-world modules/docs/tests to scrap once parity is agreed.

Change:
- compare contract fixtures against the current compiler oracle
- decide which old-world surfaces remain load-bearing versus obsolete
- create follow-up scrap tickets only after the agreed contract surface is covered

Check: no deletion happens until Compiler2 covers agreed contracts, and the remaining comparison work is explicitly about surface/contract parity rather than backend wiring.

comments.text: Compiler2 cutover decision recorded in .agent/docs/compiler2-migration.md. Backend/type/native seams are no longer blockers; old fz remains the oracle until agreed source/contract fixture coverage is represented by fz2 matrix paths or explicitly scoped out. Promoted lambda_sugars and operator_sugars to fz2-run/fz2-interp/fz2-build after verifying their matrix paths. Captured the sweep signal: 88 fixtures pass fz2 run/interp, 9 mismatch without fz2 failure, 21 have an fz2 failure. Created follow-up epic fz-rh2.16 with coverage, golden, source-surface, callable/protocol/Enum, runtime/interpreter, and final deletion-planning tickets; fz-rh2.15 remains a dependency for native substrate cleanup.

title: Compiler2 migration: compare contracts and plan old compiler scrap
